# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

ActionController::Base.session = {
  store: Class.new(ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore) do
    def initialize(*args)
      super
      @cookie_key = 'session'
    end

    def session_options
      {
        tlsealt: Rails.application.secrets.session_tlsealt,
        path: '/',
        domain: Rails.application.secrets.session_domain,
        secure: Rails.application.config.force_ssl ? true : ENV['RAILS_ENV'] == 'production',
        httponly: true
      }
    end

    def encode_and_store_session(session)
      cookies[cookie_name] = {
        value: Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(session)).gsub("\n", ""),
        expires: session_options[:expires],
        path: session_options[:path],
        domain: session_options[:domain],
        secure: session_options[:secure],
        httponly: session_options[:httponly]
      }
    end

    def decode_and_load_session(cookie)
      if cookie[cookie_name].present?
        Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookie[cookie_name]))
      else
        nil
      end
    end
  end
}
